label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if following these steps brings me back to the starting point. Let me break down the instructions step by step. First, take 3 steps. Let's assume I'm facing forward in a straight line. So after 3 steps, I'm 3 units away from the starting point in that direction. Then, take 7 more steps. So adding to the previous 3, that's 3 + 7 = 10 steps in the same direction. Now I'm 10 units away from the start. Next, turn around. Turning around means I'm now facing the opposite direction. So if I was going forward, now I'm facing backward. Then, take 10 steps. Since I turned around, each step I take now is in the opposite direction. So 10 steps backward (or forward, depending on how you look at it after turning). If I was 10 units away from the start in the original direction, and then take 10 steps in the opposite direction, that would bring me back to the starting point. Because 10 units forward minus 10 units backward equals zero. Wait, but let me check again. First 3 steps forward, then 7 steps forward (total 10). Then turn around (face backward) and take 10 steps backward relative to my current direction. Since I was facing forward and then turned around to face backward, taking 10 steps in that backward direction would actually be 10 steps in the original forward direction? No, wait. If you face forward, then turn around, you're facing backward. Then taking steps in the direction you're facing (backward) would mean moving in the opposite direction from where you started. So if you were at +10 on a number line, turning around makes you face the negative direction. Then taking 10 steps in that direction would take you to +10 - 10 =
